Wreck-It Ralph (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $189,412,677Details
International Box Office $307,098,844
Worldwide Box Office $496,511,521
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $70,625,547
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $59,911,841
Total $130,537,388
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Wreck-It Ralph longs to be as beloved as his game's perfect Good Guy, Fix-It Felix. Problem is, nobody loves a Bad Guy. But they do love heroes... so when a modern, first-person shooter game arrives featuring tough-as-nails Sergeant Calhoun, Ralph sees it as his ticket to heroism and happiness. He sneaks into the game with a simple plan - win a medal - but soon wrecks everything, and accidentally unleashes a deadly enemy that threatens every game in the arcade. Ralph's only hope? Vanellope von Schweetz, a young troublemaking "glitch" from a candy-coated cart racing game who might just be the one to teach Ralph what it means to be a Good Guy. But will he realize he is good enough to become a hero before it's "Game Over" for the entire arcade?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$49,038,712 (25.9% of total gross)
Legs:3.86 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:38.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$165,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,752 opening theaters/3,752 max. theaters, 8.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $284,173,212

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 2nd, 2012 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: November 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Hungary)
November 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Lebanon)
November 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Mexico)
November 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Philippines)
November 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
Video Release: March 5th, 2013 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some rude humor and mild action/violence.
(Rating bulletin 2243, 10/3/2012)
Running Time: 101 minutes
Franchise: Wreck-It Ralph
Cast, crew, or production detail: Directing Yourself, Epilogue, Gratuitous Cameos, Prologue, Villain's Point of View, Voiceover/Narration

Subgenre: Family Adventure

Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Wreck-It Ralph longs to be as beloved as his game's perfect Good Guy, Fix-It Felix. Problem is, nobody loves a Bad Guy. But they do love heroes... so when a modern, first-person shooter game arrives featuring tough-as-nails Sergeant Calhoun, Ralph sees it as his ticket to heroism and happiness. He sneaks into the game with a simple plan - win a medal - but soon wrecks everything, and accidentally unleashes a deadly enemy that threatens every game in the arcade. Ralph's only hope? Vanellope von Schweetz, a young troublemaking "glitch" from a candy-coated cart racing game who might just be the one to teach Ralph what it means to be a Good Guy. But will he realize he is good enough to become a hero before it's "Game Over" for the entire arcade?
